page: Microsoft Azure L24aos_v4 is a storage and memory-focused virtual machine belonging to the Laosv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with 24 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 12 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor operating at 3.7 GHz. The system features 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. A key hardware characteristic is its massive local storage capacity of 17,279 GB, which is Premium Storage capable. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 with VNNI and BF16 support, AES, and SHA_NI cryptography extensions, but lacks AMX capabilities.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core and multi-core capabilities. The L24aos_v4 achieves top 25% performance in stress-ng single-core tests, Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores, and bzip3 single-threaded decompression. Image and text processing workloads also rank in the top 25%. In contrast, multi-core stress-ng performance, memory bandwidth, and Passmark CPU and database metrics fall into the middle 50% average tier. For language model tasks, the server demonstrates top 10% performance in text generation for small 135M models, but drops to the bottom 25% for prompt processing on large 70B models, reflecting a clear performance tradeoff when scaling to larger parameters without GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the L24aos_v4 offers high resource density, combining substantial memory with an exceptionally large local storage allocation on a 24-vCPU footprint. This makes it highly suitable for storage-intensive and database workloads that require large local scratch space or high-capacity caching, such as relational databases, big data analytics, and memory-intensive data processing. However, the lack of a dedicated GPU limits its utility for heavy deep learning training, and the absence of complimentary public IPv4 addresses requires external networking configuration for public-facing deployments.
description: Microsoft Azure L24aos_v4 is a storage and memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 24 dedicated vCPUs and 12 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz. It features 192.0 GB of RAM and a substantial 17,279 GB of Premium Storage-capable local storage. Benchmarks show strong single-core and multi-core performance, placing in the top 25% for Geekbench and stress-ng single-core tests, and the top 10% for small-model LLM text generation. However, memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the average tier, and large-model LLM processing is weak due to the lack of GPU acceleration. This server is structurally optimized for storage-heavy databases, big data analytics, and memory-intensive workloads requiring massive local storage capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L24aos_v4 is a storage-optimized server with 24 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 17,279 GB local storage, delivering strong single-core and small-model LLM performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L24aos_v4 features 24 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 17.2 TB local storage for database and storage-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity storage and memory-optimized platform featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and over seventeen terabytes of local storage.
bullet_points: 24 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor at 3.7 GHz.
192.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core.
17,279 GB of Premium Storage-capable local storage.
Strong top 25% performance in single-core and multi-core Geekbench tests.
Top 10% text generation speed for small-scale language models.
Well-suited for large-scale databases, big data analytics, and heavy caching.
